editors.name: NPC Editor
editors.description: Create and manage Non-Player Characters for your game. NPCs can have dialogue, behaviors, sprites, and AI patterns.
editors.howToUse: Open NPC Editor → Click 'New NPC' → Fill name, sprite, position → Assign behavior → Link dialogue → Save

editors.name: Quest Editor
editors.description: Design quest lines with objectives, rewards, and progression tracking.
editors.howToUse: Open Quest Editor → New Quest → Set title and description → Add objectives → Configure rewards → Link to NPC or trigger

editors.name: Dialogue Editor
editors.description: Create branching dialogue trees for NPC conversations.
editors.howToUse: Open Dialogue Editor → New Dialogue → Add nodes → Connect with arrows → Set conditions → Link to NPC

editors.name: Script Editor
editors.description: Write game logic and custom behaviors using JavaScript.
editors.howToUse: Open Script Editor → New Script → Write JavaScript code → Save → Script runs in game

editors.name: Item Editor
editors.description: Create items, equipment, consumables, and collectibles.
editors.howToUse: Open Item Editor → New Item → Set name and type → Configure stats → Add icon → Define use effect

editors.name: Enemy Editor
editors.description: Design enemy types with combat stats and AI behaviors.
editors.howToUse: Open Enemy Editor → New Enemy → Set stats → Choose AI behavior → Configure loot → Set spawn conditions

editors.name: Character Editor
editors.description: Define player characters and their progression systems.
editors.howToUse: Open Character Editor → New Character → Set base stats → Define level curve → Assign skills → Set starting gear

editors.name: Skill Editor
editors.description: Create abilities, spells, and special moves.
editors.howToUse: Open Skill Editor → New Skill → Set name and effect → Configure cost → Add animation → Set unlock conditions

editors.name: Achievement Editor
editors.description: Set up achievements and progression tracking.
editors.howToUse: Open Achievement Editor → New Achievement → Set title and icon → Define unlock condition → Add reward

editors.name: Interactive Cutscene Editor
editors.description: Create cinematic sequences with camera movements and events.
editors.howToUse: Open Cutscene Editor → New Cutscene → Add timeline events → Position camera → Add dialogue → Set triggers

editors.name: Campaign Editor
editors.description: Orchestrate game flow, levels, and story progression.
editors.howToUse: Open Campaign Editor → New Campaign → Define chapters → Link levels → Set story triggers → Configure save points

editors.name: Menu Editor
editors.description: Design custom game menus and UI screens.
editors.howToUse: Open Menu Editor → New Menu → Add UI elements → Configure actions → Style appearance → Set navigation

editors.name: Logic Editor
editors.description: Create visual logic flows and game rules.
editors.howToUse: Open Logic Editor → New Logic → Add logic nodes → Connect flows → Set conditions → Link to game events

editors.name: Algorithm Editor
editors.description: Design complex algorithms and procedural systems.
editors.howToUse: Open Algorithm Editor → Select template → Configure parameters → Test in sandbox → Apply to game

editors.name: Behavior Editor
editors.description: Create AI behavior trees for NPCs and enemies.
editors.howToUse: Open Behavior Editor → New Behavior → Add behavior nodes → Configure priorities → Set transitions → Test AI

editors.name: Prefab Editor
editors.description: Create reusable game object templates.
editors.howToUse: Open Prefab Editor → New Prefab → Add components → Set default values → Save for reuse

editors.name: Localization Editor
editors.description: Manage translations and multi-language support.
editors.howToUse: Open Localization Editor → Add languages → Create text IDs → Provide translations → Export for use

editors.name: ISO Editor
editors.description: Design isometric tile-based worlds.
editors.howToUse: Open ISO Editor → Load tileset → Paint tiles → Set heights → Add objects → Configure collision

editors.name: Pixel Editor
editors.description: Create and edit pixel art assets.
editors.howToUse: Open Pixel Editor → New sprite → Draw with tools → Add animation frames → Export

editors.name: FX Editor
editors.description: Design visual effects and particle systems.
editors.howToUse: Open FX Editor → New Effect → Configure emitter → Adjust parameters → Preview → Save

editors.name: Background Editor
editors.description: Create parallax backgrounds and skyboxes.
editors.howToUse: Open Background Editor → Add layers → Set parallax speed → Position elements → Add weather

editors.name: Shader Editor
editors.description: Create custom visual shaders and effects.
editors.howToUse: Open Shader Editor → New Shader → Write GLSL code → Set parameters → Preview → Apply to objects

editors.name: Input Editor
editors.description: Configure keyboard, mouse, and gamepad controls.
editors.howToUse: Open Input Editor → Define actions → Map keys → Configure gamepad → Test inputs → Save scheme

projectStructure.description: Projects are organized with dunyalar/ (worlds), muzikler/ (music), data/ (logic), and sprites.js
projectStructure.content:
Each project in RedGlitch has a standard structure:
- dunyalar/ - World and map data
- muzikler/ - Audio files
- data/ - Game logic and configuration
- sprites.js - Custom sprite definitions
- redglitch.json - Project metadata

engineTypes.description: RedGlitch supports three engine types: rpg-topdown, platformer-2d, and iso-pixel
engineTypes.content:
Choose your engine type in redglitch.json:
- rpg-topdown: Classic 2D RPG with top-down view
- platformer-2d: Side-scrolling platformer with physics
- iso-pixel: Isometric pixel art games

buildingGames.description: Build your game for multiple platforms
buildingGames.content:
Use 'npm run build:game "ProjectName" [platform]' where platform is:
- electron: Desktop executable
- android: Android APK
- all: Build for all platforms
